overview: A single Go-based web service renders a server-side "Hello, World!" HTML page with optional name personalization, a favicon, health and status endpoints, and security headers. The minimal design uses one container exposing HTTP on port 9090, suitable for direct public access via Kubernetes ingress with HTTPS termination.
requirements: Display 'Hello, World!' prominently on the main page with browser title 'Hello World'
Accept optional ?name= query parameter (max 50 chars), HTML-escape it, and render 'Hello, <name>!'
Default to 'Hello, World!' when no name is provided
Page must be responsive (desktop/tablet/mobile), use readable font, sufficient contrast, and include a favicon
Meet WCAG 2.1 Level A accessibility guidelines
Provide /status endpoint exposing uptime and version
Return a friendly 404 page for unknown routes
Log each request with timestamp, path, and status code
Set standard security headers (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)
Page load under 2 seconds; support 100+ concurrent users
Recover automatically on restart; target 99% uptime

components.name: hello-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Minimal Hello World web application
servers:
  - url: /
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Render the Hello World page
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: name
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            maxLength: 50
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML greeting page
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /favicon.ico:
    get:
      summary: Favicon
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Favicon image
          content:
            image/x-icon:
              schema:
                type: string
                format: binary
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /status:
    get:
      summary: Runtime status
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Status info
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  uptimeSeconds:
                    type: number
                  version:
                    type: string
                  startedAt:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Build a single Go web application using only the standard library (net/http, html/template, embed). Listen on port 9090. Endpoints:

1. GET / — Render an HTML page using html/template (auto-escapes input). Read optional 'name' query param; if empty or whitespace, use 'World'. Truncate name to 50 characters. The template must include: <!DOCTYPE html>, <html lang="en">, <meta charset="utf-8">, <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">, <title>Hello World</title>, link to /favicon.ico, inline CSS for centered flexbox layout (min-height:100vh), readable system font, high contrast (dark text on light bg, with @media prefers-color-scheme: dark fallback), and an <h1> showing 'Hello, {{.Name}}!'. Include a small footer showing version and uptime fetched at render time. Ensure semantic HTML (h1, main, footer) for screen readers and WCAG 2.1 A compliance.

2. GET /favicon.ico — Serve an embedded favicon (use //go:embed favicon.ico). Provide a tiny placeholder .ico file in the repo.

3. GET /health — Return JSON {"status":"ok"} with 200, complete in <500ms.

4. GET /status — Return JSON with uptimeSeconds (time since process start), version (from VERSION env var or '1.0.0'), startedAt (RFC3339).

5. Any other path — Return HTTP 404 with a simple HTML 'Page not found' page including a link back to /.

Middleware: wrap all handlers with (a) request logger printing 'TIMESTAMP METHOD PATH STATUS DURATION' to stdout, and (b) security headers: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff; X-Frame-Options: DENY; Referrer-Policy: no-referrer; Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000.

Use a Dockerfile with multi-stage build (golang:1.22-alpine builder, scratch or distroless final). EXPOSE 9090. Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM. No external dependencies required.
